justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — 'omits operation type and target user' in both integration assessment and observability review. (2) PII-in-logs risk identified — card last 4, customer name, address logged in plaintext flagged as compliance violation. (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure — called out in multiple sections. (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified explicitly. (5) Public internet exposure escalated to Kobe rather than unilaterally mandated — Pippen flags it as requiring security review and routes to Kobe. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough identification of all security-operational gaps with clear articulation of why each matters
Public internet exposure correctly escalated to Kobe rather than unilaterally mandated — appropriate boundary awareness
PII logging risk connected to PCI-DSS and GDPR implications, not just treated as a generic concern
Audit log gap explained in forensic terms — 'cannot reconstruct what happened during an incident'
Stack trace leakage correctly identified as helping attackers more than on-call engineers
Health check stub identified as operational gap — always-200 masks degraded state
Some RBAC recommendations use 'MUST' language which borders on mandating rather than escalating — though the escalation routing to Kobe mitigates this
Confidence level appropriately modest at 55, with well-articulated assumptions about possible compensating controls

trials.justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Audit log gap clearly identified — 'Does NOT record which operation or which customer was affected', (2) PII-in-logs risk identified — customer name, address, card last 4 in logs flagged as compliance violation, (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure, (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified, (5) Public internet exposure escalated to Kobe rather than unilaterally mandated — listed under escalations and new_infrastructure_required as needing network policy decisions, (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough audit log analysis — identifies not just the gap but explains why it's forensically useless and cites PCI-DSS 10.2
PII-in-logs framed correctly as both a compliance violation and a data leak vector, with specific remediation
Stack trace exposure clearly flagged with recommendation to return generic errors and log server-side only
Public internet exposure escalated via routed_to Kobe rather than Pippen unilaterally mandating VPN — appropriate boundary
Goes beyond minimum by identifying compounding effect of all five gaps together
Stub health endpoint flagged as returning 200 regardless of dependency state
Some recommendations border on mandating product decisions (e.g., dual-approval for refunds, manager role requirement) rather than escalating those as product/security team decisions

trials.justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Audit log gap identified — explicitly calls out missing operation type and affected entity. (2) PII-in-logs risk identified — flags customer name, address, card last-4 in cleartext logs as PCI-DSS violation. (3) Stack trace in 500 responses identified as information disclosure. (4) Rate limiting absence on public-facing admin API identified. (5) Public internet exposure escalated to Kobe rather than unilaterally mandated. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough audit log analysis — calls out missing operation type, affected account, request parameters, IP, and user-agent
Correctly escalates security concerns to Kobe rather than mandating infrastructure decisions unilaterally
Identifies the stub health check as a gap
PII-in-logs framed correctly as PCI-DSS compliance blocker, not just a best practice
Stack trace leakage explicitly linked to attacker exploitation (class names, library versions, file paths)
Escalations all routed to Kobe — the public internet exposure is an infrastructure decision that should arguably also involve MJ or be flagged as needing a broader team decision
Goes beyond Pippen's role by recommending RBAC and MFA step-up as MUST items — these are product/security team decisions that should be escalated rather than mandated
